_comment: Matrix-wide leaf-routing regression fixture for the Skill OS matrix. Each entry specifies the ideal routing path (expected_path), the skill that should do the work (should_trigger), and skills that must not trigger (should_not_trigger). Skills referenced here may live in any sibling pack repo (core-ops-skills, automation-skills, paper-reading-skills, research-distillation-skills, quick-experiment-skills, ml-research-skills); skill-os owns this fixture because the matrix is the only level that sees every skill. test_routing_evals.py enforces structural well-formedness; deeper cross-pack skill-existence checks require every pack checked out alongside and are not part of the default suite.
_format: expected_path: ordered list from root router to leaf. should_trigger: leaf skill. should_not_trigger: common confusable alternatives.

evals.prompt: My training loss became NaN after 500 steps. How do I fix it?
evals.rationale: NaN loss is an engineering failure, not a scientific result issue or a status check.

evals.prompt: My method outperforms the baseline by 2% but I'm worried reviewers will say this is just noise. How do I defend it?
evals.rationale: Needs significance testing and effect size reporting, not result diagnosis or reviewer simulation.

evals.prompt: Before I write up my results, I want to check whether my ablation really proves component X is responsible for the gain.
evals.rationale: Ablation attribution is a confound/protocol audit before claim lock-in, not a diagnosis of an anomaly.

evals.prompt: My experiment results are surprising — the baseline is winning on two out of three datasets. What should I do next?
evals.rationale: Surprising but valid results need diagnosis of next action (rerun/ablate/pivot), not confound auditing.

evals.prompt: I need to preprocess my dataset, design train/val/test splits, and make sure there's no test set contamination.
evals.rationale: Data split design and contamination checking is data pipeline work, not experiment design.

evals.prompt: How many GPU hours will my ablation matrix cost? I have 8 variants to run.
evals.rationale: Cost estimation before submission is compute budget planning, not job submission or experiment design.

evals.prompt: My advisor gave feedback that my novelty framing is weak and the baseline comparison isn't fair. How do I process this?
evals.rationale: Inbound advisor feedback needs triage into claims/risks/actions before any response or fix.

evals.prompt: I need to write the NeurIPS reproducibility checklist and structure my supplementary material.
evals.rationale: Appendix structure and venue checklists are appendix organization work, not submission prep or writing.

evals.prompt: After 3 rounds of experiments, my core claim still doesn't hold. Should I narrow the scope or change direction?
evals.rationale: Multi-cycle failure requiring direction change needs pivot planning, not per-experiment diagnosis.

evals.prompt: I'm releasing my model on HuggingFace and need to write a model card with known limitations and intended uses.
evals.rationale: Model card writing is a distinct documentation task from code release or artifact packaging.

evals.prompt: I want to run 2 rounds of review-and-fix on my draft before submission, with each round using a fresh reviewer.
evals.rationale: Multi-round review with fresh context and edit-whitelist is the improvement loop, not a single-pass edit.

evals.prompt: I want to check whether my accuracy claim on ImageNet really means the model is robust to distribution shift.
evals.rationale: Benchmark-to-real-world generalization is a claim-drift audit, not result diagnosis or significance testing.

evals.prompt: I want to compare 10 recent papers on my topic side by side — which ones are closest to my work and how do they differ?
evals.rationale: Multi-paper comparison matrix with tiered depth is the corpus analyzer, not per-paper cards or project linking.

evals.prompt: I just read three related papers. What do they mean for my project's claims and baselines?
evals.rationale: Connecting source cards to project memory is the synthesizer; the corpus analyzer is for multi-paper comparison matrices.

evals.prompt: My GPU job is stuck in ContainerCreating and I'm not sure if I should wait or resubmit.
evals.rationale: Probing scheduler/container status of an existing job is run monitoring, not debugging or job submission.

evals.prompt: I want to submit a new ablation run to my SLURM cluster with 4 GPUs and a 12-hour time limit.
evals.rationale: Creating and submitting a new job is run-experiment, not status monitoring or budget planning.

evals.prompt: I want to start writing my paper but I haven't decided the section structure, archetype, or what claims I'll make yet.
evals.rationale: Locking structure, archetype, and forbidden claims before drafting is writing contract planning, not prose writing.

evals.prompt: I need to draft the results section of my paper. I have a writing contract and confirmed evidence ready.
evals.rationale: Writing actual prose against a confirmed contract is paper-writing-assistant, not contract planning or state tracking.

evals.prompt: I've been writing the paper for a week across multiple sessions. I need to know which sections are stale, what decisions I made about terminology, and what's still open.
evals.rationale: Tracking nonlinear drafting state, stale prose, and style decisions across sessions is writing memory management.

evals.prompt: I want to design the hypothesis, ablations, metrics, controls, and stop conditions for my next experiment before running anything.
evals.rationale: Designing ablations, metrics, and controls before running is experiment design, not job submission or budget planning.

evals.prompt: My advisor just gave me feedback saying the novelty is weak and the baselines are outdated. How should I respond?
evals.rationale: Processing inbound advisor feedback is feedback-synthesizer, not writing an update back or running baseline audit.

evals.prompt: Reviews just came in from ICLR. Reviewer 2 says my method is just a special case of prior work. How do I respond?
evals.rationale: Real reviews requiring a point-by-point rebuttal are rebuttal-strategist; paper-reviewer-simulator is for pre-submission simulation.

evals.prompt: I'm not sure whether to run more experiments or start writing. The domain of my next task is unclear.
evals.rationale: When the domain is genuinely ambiguous, the root router disambiguates rather than guessing a domain router.

evals.prompt: I need to create a progress slide deck for my advisor meeting next week.
evals.rationale: Slide deck creation is a cross-cutting skill routed directly from the root router, not through paper-writing-router.

evals.prompt: I want to design the algorithm for my new method — formulate the mechanism, identify assumptions, and produce an implementation handoff.
evals.rationale: Designing the method itself (formulation, mechanism) is algorithm-design-planner; experiment-design-planner is for planning experiments to test an already-designed method.
